q: In this chapter, the range of audibility, infrasound and ultrasound together with their applications will be studied. Echo, reverberation and ___ effect are also discussed.
The propagation of ___ is a learning outcome expected of students.
Students are expected to identify ___ frequencies as a learning outcome.
Investigating infrasound, ultrasound and their ___ is a learning outcome for students.
Students are expected to describe the occurrence of ___.
Examining and explaining the ___ effect is a learning outcome.
Differentiating between pitch and ___ of sound is a learning outcome for students.
Sound is a sequence of pressure waves which propagates through ___ media such as air or water.
During their propagation, sound waves can be reflected, refracted or ___ by the medium.
A relationship between density and ___ is a property of media which affects sound propagation.
The relationship between density and pressure, affected by temperature, determines the speed of sound within the ___.
The ___ of the medium determines the rate at which sound is attenuated.
For many media, such as air or water, attenuation due to viscosity is ___.
Speed of a sound wave can be affected by the density, temperature, and ___ of the medium.
The speed of sound will be different for different ___.
In a solid medium, the speed of sound $v$ is given by $v = \sqrt{\frac{Y}{\rho}}$, where $Y$ is Young's modulus and $\rho$ is the ___ of the solid material.
In liquids, the velocity of sound $v$ is given by a similar expression $v = \sqrt{\frac{B}{\rho}}$, where $B$ is the ___ modulus.
In liquids, the velocity of sound $v$ is given by a similar expression $v = \sqrt{\frac{B}{\rho}}$, where $\rho$ is density of ___.
In gases, the velocity of sound $v$ is given by $v = \sqrt{\frac{\gamma p}{\rho}}$, where $\gamma$ is the adiabatic constant of gas, $p$ is ___ in gas and $\rho$ is its density.
Longitudinal sound waves travel faster in solids than they do in liquids than they do in ___.
Young's modulus and the bulk modulus are elasticity properties of the ___.
The SI units of Young's modulus are ___.
The SI units of the bulk modulus are ___.

The level of intensity of a sound is measured relative to a reference intensity value $I_o$, taken as 10\textsuperscript{-12} W m\textsuperscript{-2}, which corresponds to the minimum intensity of sound that can be heard (___ of hearing or minimum audible intensity).
The decibel (dB) is the ___ used for measuring sound intensity.
The intensity level of sound $\beta$ in dB is defined by the equation $\beta \text{ (dB)} = 10 \text{log}_{10} \frac{I}{I_o}$, where $I$ is the intensity of sound and $I_o$ is the ___ intensity.
For every ___ dB added to the sound level, the loudness of the sound is increased by 10 times.
120 dB sound will be ___ times louder than 100 dB sound.
Sound intensity is a direct measure of the power reaching an ___ measured in W m\textsuperscript{-2}.
The speed of sound is higher in ___ than liquids and gases.
___ is a key word related to sound propagation and properties.
___ is a key word related to sound propagation and properties.
Young's ___ is a key word related to sound propagation and properties.
Bulk modulus is a key word related to sound ___ and properties.
Adiabatic ___ is a key word related to sound propagation and properties.
Elasticity is a key word related to sound ___ and properties.
Echoes are produced where a sound wave is reflected from a large and ___ surface.
An echo is repetition of sound due to the ___ of sound.
The echo can be heard separately from the original sound if the source of the sound is much ___ to the observer than to the reflecting surface.
Reflection of sound obeys the laws of ___.
To reduce the effect of echo in buildings, walls are covered up with ___.
To reduce the effect of echo in buildings, the floors are covered with rugs and ___.
Materials like padding and carpets absorb the incident sound and reduce the amount of sound reflecting off wall and floor ___.
Echo can be used to determine the ___ $d$ between a source of sound and the reflecting surface.
The formula $v = \frac{2d}{t}$ is used to determine distance, where $v$ is the speed of sound and $t$ is the ___ time.
Some animals such as bats are known to use ___ to navigate and locate their prey.
Echolocation is the special ability of emitting sounds and interpreting the ___.
A reverberation is a reflection of light or sound waves, or a wide reaching ___ of an action.
An example of a reverberation is the sound bouncing around in a large ___.
Echo is a ___ word related to this subchapter.
Echolocation is a ___ word related to this subchapter.
Reverberation is a ___ word related to this subchapter.
___ means able to be heard.
Audibility means the ability to be heard or capability of detecting sounds in a certain range of ___.
The range of frequencies a person can hear is known as the range of ___.
For humans with normal hearing, the lower limit of audibility is ___ Hz.
For humans with normal hearing, the upper limit of audibility is about ___ Hz.
The lower and upper limits of human hearing are known as the limit of ___.
Low frequency sounds which are under the lower limit of audibility (20 Hz) are called ___.
Infrasound ___ be heard by human.
A vibrating ruler can be seen but not heard its vibrating sound because the sound produced is below the lower limit of ___ of human ear.
High frequency sounds which are above the upper limit of audibility (20 000 Hz) are called ___.
Ultrasound ___ be heard by human ear.
Bats and whales use ultrasound in ___.
Most ___ technologies use ultrasound.
If you blow a dog whistle, a dog may bark in response, even though you do not hear any sound because the frequency is above the upper limit of audibility of humans but within that of ___.
Manufacturers of concrete use ultrasound to check for cracks or ___.
Ultrasound can be used to inspect metal pipes and measure the thickness of ___ boards.
A prenatal ultrasound test uses ___ sound waves, inaudible to the human ear.
In a prenatal ultrasound test, sound waves are transmitted through the abdomen via a device called a ___.
With prenatal ultrasound, the echoes are recorded and transformed into video or photographic images of the ___.
Ultrasonic scalers use ultrasound to remove calculus (plaque) deposits from the ___.
Ultrasonic scalers work mechanically using high frequency ultrasound that effectively blasts ___.
Plaque bacteria and calculus can be flushed from teeth by a small jet of water or ___ mouthwash.
The transmission of high energy ultrasound may result in the creation of ___ bubbles.
Cavitation bubbles are created at sites of ___.
Cavitation bubbles may displace ___ from surfaces during ultrasonic cleaning.
Ultrasonic cleaning is especially effective in cleaning irregular surfaces or internal ___ and passageways.
The sonar (sound navigation and ranging) is a type of technology that works based on ___.
Sonar is used by ships for navigation at sea and to detect the position of other ___.
The depth of the sea or the position of shoals of fish can be found by sending out a signal (a pulse of sound) from a ___.
Sonar determines depth by noting the time interval for the ___ signal (the echo) to be received.
Elephants communicate over a long distance using sound with fundamental frequencies as low as ___ Hz.
A rhinoceros uses frequencies down to ___ Hz for communication.
Infrasound waves (f < 20 Hz) have long wavelengths, and are thus able to travel over long ___.
Elephants are able to talk using frequencies as low as 15 Hz over long distances (up to ___ km).
Rhinoceros can communicate over long distances further than 4 km because the lowest frequency they produced is 10 Hz which is less than 15 Hz and longer ___.
Infrasound waves with lower frequency and longer wavelengths are able to travel longer distances with less ___.
Infrasound cannot be heard by humans because its frequency is below the lower limit of ___.
Range of audibility is a ___ word for this section.
Infrasound is a ___ word for this section.
Ultrasound is a ___ word for this section.
The Doppler effect or Doppler shift is the change in ___ of a wave related to an observer.
The Doppler effect is due to the ___ motion between the source and observer.
The Doppler effect is named after the Austrian physicist Christian ___, who described the phenomenon in 1842.
A common example of Doppler shift is the change of frequency (pitch) heard when a vehicle sounding a ___ approaches and recedes from an observer.
One case of Doppler effect is when the ___ is stationary and the source is moving towards or away from the observer.
Another case of Doppler effect is when the source is stationary and the ___ is moving towards or away from the source.
The third case of Doppler effect is when both the source and observer are moving ___ to each other.
The Doppler equation can be expressed in general form as $f_o = f_s \left( \frac{v \pm v_o}{v \mp v_s} \right)$, where $f_o$ is frequency heard by the ___.
In the Doppler equation, $f_s$ is the frequency produced by the ___.
In the Doppler equation, $v$ is the speed of sound in the ___.
In the Doppler equation, $v_o$ is the speed of the ___.
In the Doppler equation, $v_s$ is the speed of the ___.
When the source and observer are approaching relative to each other, the observed frequency $f_o$ is ___ than the source frequency $f_s$.
When the source and observer are receding relative to each other, the observed frequency $f_o$ is ___ than the source frequency $f_s$.
In the Doppler equation, $+v_o$ is used when the observer moves ___ the source.
In the Doppler equation, $-v_o$ is used when the observer moves ___ from the source.
In the Doppler equation, $-v_s$ is used when the source moves ___ the observer.
In the Doppler equation, $+v_s$ is used when the source moves ___ from the observer.
When observer and source are stationary, $v_o = v_s = ___$.
Doppler effect is observed not only in sound but also in ___ radiations.
Applications of Doppler effect include radio wave, microwave, and ___.
The wavelength of light from a star shifts towards the red end of the spectrum (lower frequency, longer wavelength) when the earth and the star are moving ___ from each other.
The phenomenon where light from a star shifts towards the red end of the spectrum is called ___.
If an astronomical body were approaching the earth, the spectral lines would shift towards the ___ end.
Radar (radio detection and ranging) is a form of ___ that uses electromagnetic waves.
The Doppler effect is used in some types of radar to measure the ___ of detected objects.
Examples of radar that use the Doppler effect include police radar, air traffic control radar and ___ radar.
Doppler ultrasound is a technique used to examine ___ flow.
Blood flow rates can be found by measuring Doppler shift in frequency of ultrasound reflected by ___ blood cell.
The technique of measuring Doppler shift in frequency of ultrasound reflected by red blood cell is known as ___.
Doppler ultrasound can help reveal blockage to blood flow and show the formation of ___ in arteries.
Doppler ultrasound can provide detailed information on the heartbeat of the ___ during labor and delivery.
Doppler ultrasound can be used to examine organs such as the heart (echocardiography) and ___.
Doppler effect is a ___ word for this section.
Red shift is a ___ word for this section.
Radar is a ___ word for this section.
Doppler ultrasound is a ___ word for this section.
The ___ of the note (how high it is) is determined by its frequency.
The greater the frequency, the ___ is the pitch.
The loudness of the note depends upon the ___ of the wave that produces it.
The greater the amplitude, the ___ the note.
More ___ is used to produce a larger amplitude, resulting in a louder note.
If the amplitude of a sound wave is twice that of another, its loudness is ___ times greater.
Quality of a sound is the characteristic which distinguishes two sounds of the same loudness and same pitch but emitted by two different ___.
The timbre of a sound depends on its ___, which varies with the number of overtones, or harmonics.
Notes produced by the violin are richer than others because it has more ___.
The intensity of the sound at a place is the energy transmitted per second per unit area held normally to the direction of ___.
Intensity of sound $I$ can be expressed as $I = \frac{1}{2} \rho v \omega^2 A^2$, where $\rho$ is density of ___.
In the intensity formula $I = \frac{1}{2} \rho v \omega^2 A^2$, $v$ is the ___ of sound wave.
In the intensity formula $I = \frac{1}{2} \rho v \omega^2 A^2$, $\omega$ is the ___ frequency of sound wave.
In the intensity formula $I = \frac{1}{2} \rho v \omega^2 A^2$, $A$ is the ___ of sound wave.
The intensity of a wave is directly proportional to the ___ of the amplitude of the wave ($I \propto A^2$).
___ is a sensation and hence, unlike intensity, it depends on the observer.
To the human ears, the loudness of the sound is ___.
Normally the greater the intensity, the greater the ___ of the sound is.
Pitch is a ___ word for this section.
Loudness is a ___ word for this section.
Quality of sound (timbre) is a ___ word for this section.
Audibility means the ability to be heard or capability of detecting sounds in certain range of ___.
Timbre is the quality of sound which allows us to distinguish between different sound sources producing sound at the same pitch and ___.

The speed of sound in a particular liquid is 1.6\times10\textsuperscript{3} m s\textsuperscript{-1}. If the bulk modulus of the liquid is 0.15 \times10\textsuperscript{10} N m\textsuperscript{-2}, what is the ___ of the liquid?
An astronaut on the Moon's surface is unable to hear the sound of the rocket thrusters of his lunar lander craft because there is no ___ on the moon's surface.
Ultrasound is used in medical imaging rather than sound waves of audible frequencies because it provides better ___.
To get better resolution, ___ frequencies ultrasound are used but at the expense of less penetration.
Sound waves are attenuated within the distance of about 50 $\lambda$ in ___.
The velocity of sound in soft tissue is 1500 m s\textsuperscript{-1} and mean depth of thyroid is 0.75 cm. What is the most suitable frequency of ultrasound to diagnose ___?
